title_en: Importance for Students and Available Editions
title_id: Pentingnya bagi Pelajar dan Edisi-edisi yang Tersedia
content_en:
Ash-Sharh al-Kabir is essential for students of Maliki jurisprudence who want to understand the madhab at an advanced level. It is typically studied after the student has worked through Aqrab al-Masalik (ad-Dardir's own introductory text) and the Risala of Ibn Abi Zayd, which together provide the foundation needed to engage with the more detailed analysis of the Sharh al-Kabir. The commentary is most productively read alongside ad-Dasuqi's hashiya, which provides additional explanation and extension of ad-Dardir's analysis.

For students in North Africa and West Africa, where the Maliki madhab is dominant, ash-Sharh al-Kabir is a curriculum text in traditional Islamic educational institutions. It is assigned in advanced fiqh classes and used as the primary reference for fatwa production. Students who complete a thorough study of the Sharh al-Kabir with its hashiya have the most comprehensive available overview of the Maliki tradition.

For researchers in Islamic law, the Sharh al-Kabir is an important primary source for the Maliki tradition in the eighteenth century and for the history of Islamic legal education at al-Azhar. It documents the state of Maliki jurisprudence at a critical period in the tradition's history, when Egyptian Maliki scholarship was reaching its most refined development.

The standard printed edition of the Sharh al-Kabir is published together with ad-Dasuqi's hashiya in a four-volume set, making the combined work more accessible than reading the two texts separately. This combined edition has been published by Dar Ihya al-Kutub al-Arabiyya and widely reprinted. The complete text is available in digital repositories. Students are strongly encouraged to use the combined edition so that ad-Dasuqi's additions are immediately visible when reading ad-Dardir's text. The layered format — with Mukhtasar Khalil as the base text, ad-Dardir's Sharh as the primary commentary, and ad-Dasuqi's hashiya as the outer ring of annotation — illustrates the cumulative way in which Islamic legal knowledge was transmitted and refined across generations. Working through this layered text develops not only mastery of Maliki jurisprudence but a feel for how the scholarly tradition itself functioned, which is an education of enduring value for anyone engaged in serious Islamic learning.
content_id:
Al-Syarh al-Kabir sangat penting bagi pelajar fikih Maliki yang ingin memahami mazhab di tingkat lanjut. Kitab ini biasanya dipelajari setelah pelajar menyelesaikan Aqrab al-Masalik (teks pengantar al-Dardiri sendiri) dan Risalah Ibn Abi Zayd, yang bersama-sama memberikan fondasi yang diperlukan untuk terlibat dengan analisis Syarh al-Kabir yang lebih terperinci. Syarh ini paling produktif dibaca bersama hasyiyah al-Dasuqi, yang memberikan penjelasan tambahan dan perluasan atas analisis al-Dardiri.

Bagi pelajar di Afrika Utara dan Afrika Barat, di mana mazhab Maliki dominan, Al-Syarh al-Kabir adalah teks kurikulum di lembaga-lembaga pendidikan Islam tradisional. Kitab ini ditugaskan dalam kelas-kelas fikih tingkat lanjut dan digunakan sebagai referensi utama untuk produksi fatwa. Pelajar yang menyelesaikan studi mendalam atas Syarh al-Kabir beserta hasyiyah-nya memiliki gambaran paling komprehensif yang tersedia tentang tradisi Maliki.

Bagi peneliti hukum Islam, Syarh al-Kabir adalah sumber primer yang penting untuk tradisi Maliki pada abad kedelapan belas dan untuk sejarah pendidikan hukum Islam di Al-Azhar. Kitab ini mendokumentasikan keadaan fikih Maliki pada periode kritis dalam sejarah tradisi, ketika ilmu Maliki Mesir sedang mencapai perkembangan paling halus dan matangnya.

Edisi cetak standar Syarh al-Kabir diterbitkan bersama hasyiyah al-Dasuqi dalam satu set empat jilid, membuat karya gabungan ini lebih mudah diakses daripada membaca dua teks tersebut secara terpisah. Edisi gabungan ini telah diterbitkan oleh Dar Ihya al-Kutub al-Arabiyyah dan banyak dicetak ulang. Teks lengkap tersedia dalam repositori digital. Para pelajar sangat dianjurkan untuk menggunakan edisi gabungan agar tambahan-tambahan al-Dasuqi langsung terlihat saat membaca teks al-Dardiri. Format berlapis ini — dengan Mukhtashar Khalil sebagai teks dasar, Syarh al-Dardiri sebagai syarh utama, dan hasyiyah al-Dasuqi sebagai lapisan anotasi terluar — mengilustrasikan cara kumulatif pengetahuan hukum Islam ditransmisikan dan diperhalus lintas generasi. Menyelesaikan teks berlapis ini tidak hanya mengembangkan penguasaan fikih Maliki tetapi juga kepekaan terhadap cara kerja tradisi keilmuan itu sendiri, yang merupakan pendidikan bernilai abadi bagi siapa pun yang terlibat dalam pembelajaran Islam yang serius.
content_ar: Ash-Sharh al-Kabir is الجوهري for students of المالكي jurisprudence who want لفهم the madhab at an advanced level. It is typically studied بعد the student has worked through Aqrab al-Masalik (ad-Dardir's own introductory text) and the Risala of Ibn Abi Zayd, which together provide the foundation needed to engage with the more detailed analysis of the Sharh al-Kabir. The commentary is most productively read alongside ad-Dasuqi's hashiya, which provides additional explanation and extension of ad-Dardir's analysis. For students in North Africa and West Africa, where the المالكي madhab is dominant, ash-Sharh al-Kabir is a curriculum text in التقليدي Islamic educational institutions. It is assigned in advanced الفقه classes and used as the الأساسي reference for الفتوى production. Students who complete a thorough study of the Sharh al-Kabir with its hashiya have the most comprehensive available overview of the المالكي التراث. For researchers in Islamic law, the Sharh al-Kabir is an المهم الأساسي source for the المالكي التراث in the eighteenth century and for the history of Islamic legal education at al-Azhar. It documents the state of المالكي jurisprudence at a البالغ الأهمية period in the التراث's history, عندما Egyptian المالكي scholarship was reaching its most refined development. The standard printed edition of the Sharh al-Kabir is published together with ad-Dasuqi's hashiya in a four-volume set, making the combined work more accessible than reading the two texts separately. This combined edition has been published by Dar Ihya al-Kutub al-Arabiyya and widely reprinted. The complete text is available in digital repositories. Students are strongly encouraged to use the combined edition لذا that ad-Dasuqi's additions are immediately visible عندما reading ad-Dardir's text. The layered format — with Mukhtasar Khalil as the base text, ad-Dardir's Sharh as the الأساسي commentary, and ad-Dasuqi's hashiya as the outer ring of annotation — illustrates the cumulative الطريق in which Islamic legal العلم was transmitted and refined across generations. Working through this layered text develops not only mastery of المالكي jurisprudence but a feel for how the العلمي التراث itself functioned, which is an education of enduring value for anyone engaged in serious Islamic learning.
